etymology.note: iki itself etymologises further as i- + ki, expanded inline.

etymology.source: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Bracketing: (ci= e) + -p. The 1PL.EXC.A clitic ci= combines with e 'eat' to form a clausal VP 'we eat', which -p (NMLZ, vowel-final allomorph) nominalises → 'what we eat' → 'fish'. Surface ci+e+p contracts to cep (medial *cie reduces by glide-formation / vowel-loss). Standard etymology in Tamura 1996 and Sato 2020.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2020アイヌ語における文法的カテゴリーの転換について
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Appears as an incorporated noun in compound verbs such as e-yay-ko-tuyma-si-ram-suy-pa.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Suppletive plural counterpart is oka. The homophonous clitic =an realises an indefinite 4S argument and is a separate entry.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Plural counterpart of an.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Suppletive plural counterpart is paye. The 沙流/千歳 dialect lemma is arpa; 樺太 has oman.

composition_note: Phonological fusion of i-nukar (INDEF.OBJ + see) with loss of the medial /u/ — synchronically a one-argument verb meaning 'look (at something)'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎

composition_note: Lexicalised i-ku (INDEF.OBJ + drink), synchronically a one-argument verb 'drink alcohol'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Plural counterpart of arpa.

composition_note: Lexicalised fusion of paye + oka (go.PL + exist.PL); synchronically a one-argument verb meaning 'walk around (plural subject)'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎
notes: Anchors a productive derivation family: i-kar (INDEF.OBJ + make), e-kar (APPL + make), ko-kar (APPL.DAT + make), yay-kar (REFL + make), ekarkar (lexicalised reduplication 'do/decorate'), koykar (lexicalised 'imitate'). Also a frequent etymological component (nukar = nu 'eye' + kar; cf. 中川1995).

composition_note: Lexicalised i-kar (INDEF.OBJ + make), synchronically a one-argument verb 'make (something)'. Parallel to inkar = i-nukar 'look'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Lexicalised reduplicated e-kar; productive verb 'do (something)' / 'decorate' depending on context. The applicative e- preserves a patient slot; reduplication adds intensifying/iterative force without changing arity.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎

composition_note: Lexicalised ko-i-kar (APPL.DAT + INDEF.OBJ + make), surface koy-kar with the regular y-epenthesis between ko- and a following i-initial morpheme. Synchronically a two-argument verb 'imitate / model on'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

rules.description: Productive causative; adds an external causer (arity +1).

sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察
中川2024アイヌ語広文典
notes: Allomorph distribution: -re after vowel-final stems, -te in restricted contexts, -e elsewhere. Treated as a single morpheme for valency purposes. SRPA = Shiratori's Proto-Ainu Reconstruction.

rules.description: Productive causative; adds an external causer (arity +1). Surfaces after vowel-final stems.

sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察
中川2024アイヌ語広文典
notes: Allomorph of the productive causative -e / -re / -te; distributed by phonotactics (this -re form follows vowel-final stems such as ruska-re, iruska-re).

rules.description: Indefinite causative: the original subject is dropped from the clause (中川2024 'アリティ 0').

sources: 中川2024アイヌ語広文典
notes: Net arity change is zero: a new causer is added while the original subject is demoted out of the clause.

rules.description: Indirect reflexive: identifies the newly added causer with another argument (佐藤2007).

sources: 佐藤2007アイヌ語千歳方言の再帰接頭辞yayとsiについて

rules.description: Direct reflexive: the patient is identified with the agent (佐藤2007).

sources: 佐藤2007アイヌ語千歳方言の再帰接頭辞yayとsiについて

rules.description: Indefinite-object prefix: saturates the patient slot.

sources: 佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎

rules.description: Applicative: adds an oblique goal/recipient as an external argument.

sources: 佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎

rules.description: Reciprocal prefix u-: the patient is identified with the (plural) agent — 'each other'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎

rules.description: Direct causative (non-productive): adds a causer; only attaches to a closed class of stative/intransitive stems (e.g. uhuy → uhuy-ka, pirka → pirka-ka).

sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察
notes: Less productive than -e/-re/-te. Direct causative only: the causee is typically a non-volitional state or process and may be incorporated or anti-passivised, unlike the indirect causative formed with -e/-re/-te.

rules.description: Anti-causative/middle prefix ci-: backgrounds the agent (originally 1pl.EXC), yielding a self-acting/spontaneous reading (e.g. ci-koyki-p 'game animal', ci-sanasanke 'descend').

sources: 佐藤2023アイヌ語の動詞の結合価と3項動詞
notes: Derivational reflex of the 1pl.EXC subject clitic ci=. Productivity is limited compared with i- (INDEF.OBJ); attested mainly with stems that admit a self-acting/middle reading.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Bound-like nominaliser/agent suffix in many compounds (e.g. cepkoykikur 'fisherman').

composition_note: Noun-incorporation compound: cep (fish) is incorporated as the patient of koyki, leaving a one-argument verb 'to catch fish'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: Deverbal noun: cepkoyki + kur (person) → 'fisherman'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Noun-incorporation compound: aynu (person) is incorporated as the patient of koyki, yielding 'attack people'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Noun-incorporation compound: cikap (bird) + koyki → 'to shoot/catch birds'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Bound verb stem. Singular finite form is suye '揺らす'; plural is suypa. Occurs incorporated in polysynthetic compounds such as si-ram-suy-pa '心が揺れる → ponder'. Distinct from the homophonous adverb suy 'again'.

sources: 佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎
中川2024アイヌ語広文典
notes: Verbal plural suffix. Marks plural agreement on its host but does not change the argument structure, so it carries no valency rule.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川2024アイヌ語広文典
notes: Singular inflectional marker on certain bound stems (suy → suye, paye → ?). Carries no valency rule — distinct from the productive causative -e / -re / -te. The valency of this marker is treated as 0 here; the host stem's lexicalised arity is what surfaces.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川2024アイヌ語広文典
notes: Plural inflectional counterpart of -e-infl on stem-internal positions (suy → suypa). Distinct from the verbal-plural -pa applied to whole verbs; carries no valency rule.

composition_note: Transitive singular form: bound stem suy + inflectional -e. The -e here is not the productive causative; it's the singular-action inflection paired with -pa (cf. suypa). Valency contribution of suy vs -e is not crisply assigned in the literature — the database treats suy as +1 (intransitive) and the inflection as 0, but treating suy as 0 and -e as +1 would be equally defensible.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: Plural counterpart of suye: bound stem suy + inflectional -pa. The -pa here pairs with the singular -e inside the same stem; distinct from the verbal-plural -pa attached to whole verbs.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

slots.label_jp: 聞かれる・感じられる対象

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Highly polysemous sensory verb covering hear / listen / smell / sense / feel and an extended 'be effective' reading. The semantic range closely parallels Japanese 聞く (kiku) and Korean 듣다 (deutda) — likely areal calque rather than borrowing. Zero-derives to a noun 'eye' (see nu-n entry); the noun sense is no longer productive in isolation, but Nakagawa 1995 records ヌ nu² as a bound noun root.

sources: 中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
佐藤2020アイヌ語における文法的カテゴリーの転換について
notes: Zero-derived noun-root form of the sensory verb nu. Surfaces only as the first member of compounds (nu-kar 'see', nu-pe 'tear', e-nu-kor 'watch over'); not used in isolation.

etymology.source: 佐藤2020アイヌ語における文法的カテゴリーの転換について

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Free noun 'water/liquid'. Distinct from the homophonous nominaliser suffix -pe / -p that is widespread in compounds (e.g. ek-pe 'one who comes').

rules.description: Nominaliser: turns an intransitive verb into the agent / participant noun, saturating the verb's external slot.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Productive agent / participant nominaliser used after consonant-final stems. Homophonous with the noun pe 'water'; cf. sapan-pe 'crown' = sapa-un + -pe, ek-pe 'one who comes' = ek + -pe. The vowel-final allomorph is the separate -p entry (rik-o-p 'star').

rules.description: Nominaliser: turns an intransitive verb into the agent / participant noun. Vowel-final-stem allomorph of -pe.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Synchronically distinct surface form: -p, used after vowel-final stems (rik-o-p 'star', a-e-p 'food'). Same nominalising function as -pe; we list them as separate seed entries so the displayed surface in the composition tree matches the actual word (rikop = rik-o-p, not rik-o-pe → rikop).

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Locative existential verb 'X is at Y' / 'be located at'. Distinct from the homophonous case-particle (allative postposition) un. Surfaces as the inner head of incorporation compounds like sapa-un 'be at the head', pa-un 'be on top', ror-un 'be at the place of honour'.

composition_note: sapa (head) incorporated into the locative verb un — 'be at the head' / 'sit on top of one's head'. The noun incorporation saturates un's location slot, leaving a one-place predicate suitable for nominalisation with -pe.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: (sapa-un)-pe — 'the thing that is on the head' → ceremonial crown worn by men. The -pe here is the agent-nominaliser, not the homophonous noun pe 'water'. Surface fuses sapaun-pe → sapanpe by dropping the medial u.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Wiktionary JA
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Possessed-noun stem 'head', incorporated into pa-un 'be on top' and surfacing in compounds like paunpe (ceremonial crown). Distinct from the plural suffix -pa and the year-counter -pa (cf. tan-pa 'this year').

composition_note: pa (head) incorporated into the locative verb un — 'be on the head'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: (pa-un)-pe — 'the thing that is on the head'. Synonym of sapanpe.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: an (exist, vi) + -pe (NMLZ): 'the thing that exists' → 'the truth'. The -pe here is the agent-nominaliser, not the noun pe 'water'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: e- (APPL.about) + inkar (look at) + -pe (NMLZ): 'the thing one looks at / sees with' → 'eye'. The -pe here is the agent-nominaliser. Note the inner inkar already encapsulates i- + nukar, so the full historical etymology is e-(i-nukar)-pe.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: rik (high) + o (be located) + -p (NMLZ, vowel-final allomorph): 'the thing that is up high' → 'star'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: yay- (REFL) + kira (escape) + -re (CAUS, the allomorph used after vowel-final stems): 'make oneself escape' → 'flee at full speed'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Wiktionary JA
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Transitive verb 'eat (X)'. Distinct from the applicative prefix e- and the homophonous possessor pronoun e. The intransitive form ipe (i- + e 'eat something') is its indefinite-object derivation.

composition_note: i- (INDEF.OBJ) + e (eat, vt): 'eat something' → intransitive 'eat (a meal)'. Also surfaces as a noun 'a meal / food'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: e (eat) + -p (NMLZ, vowel-final allomorph): 'the thing eaten' → 'food'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Tommy 1949
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: a= (4.A / 1pl-incl agent clitic, used impersonally) + e (eat) + -p (NMLZ, vowel-final allomorph): 'the thing one eats' → 'food'. The a= clitic gives an impersonal-agent reading common to traditional Ainu nominalisations.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Tommy 1949
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: a= (4.A) + e- (APPL.with) + ipe (eat-a-meal) + -p (NMLZ, vowel-final allomorph): 'the thing one eats a meal with' → eating utensil (a chopstick / spoon / serving stick).
sources: Tommy 1949
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: a= (4.A) + e- (APPL.about) + inu (listen) + -p (NMLZ, vowel-final allomorph): 'the thing one listens to' → traditional lore / heard tradition.
sources: Tommy 1949
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: yay- (REFL) + peka (towards / along) + -re (CAUS, allomorph after vowel-final stem): 'direct oneself along ~' → 'head toward'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Wiktionary JA

composition_note: nu-n (eye, zero-derived from the sensory verb nu) + pe (water/liquid) → 'tear'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: yay- (REFL.DIR) + nukar (see) — 'see oneself' → look at oneself in a mirror / reflect upon oneself.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: yay- (REFL.DIR) + nu (sense / hear) — literally 'sense oneself' → 'think'. Parallels si-ram-suy 'one's heart shakes (within oneself)' which is also a sensory-reflexive idiom for thinking.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: i- (INDEF.OBJ) + nu (hear) — literally 'hear something' → 'listen, hear (intransitive)'. The patient slot is saturated by the indefinite-object prefix, leaving a one-argument verb.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
NINJALCorpus
notes: Base verb root that reduplicates to moymoy (iterative) and combines with the lexical causative -ke in moymoyke.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
Tommy 1949

etymology.note: Reduplicated moy intensifies / iterates motion; -ke adds the causative dimension. Lexical causative -ke is distinct from the productive -e / -re / -te.
etymology.source: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
NINJALCorpus

etymology.note: Same surface form serves as verb 'speak' and noun 'word / language' via zero-derivation — a textbook category-conversion example.
etymology.source: 佐藤2020アイヌ語における文法的カテゴリーの転換について

composition_note: e- (APPL 'about/with') + nu-n (eye, zero-derived noun-root form) + kor (have / hold). Literally 'have one's eye about ~' → 'watch over ~'. Used especially of gods watching over places or people.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: ko-（〜に対して applicative）+ i-（不定目的語 INDEF.OBJ）+ ruska（怒る）. The indefinite-object prefix saturates ruska's patient, then ko- adds an external goal — 'to be angry at something towards someone'.
glosses_en: be angry at something towards someone
glosses_jp: 誰かに対して何かに怒る
sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察

composition_note: si- (indirect reflexive) coreferences the ko-added goal with the subject — 'to be angry at something towards oneself'.
glosses_en: be angry at something towards oneself
glosses_jp: 自分に対して何かに怒る
sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察

composition_note: i- (INDEF.OBJ) + ruska (be angry at) — the patient is saturated by the indefinite-object prefix, leaving an intransitive 'be angry (at something)'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察

composition_note: Not an independently attested standalone morpheme in modern Ainu — surfaces only inside iruskare / koiruskare / ukoiruskare etc. Listed by Batchelor 1938 (hence the Tommy 1949 ingest pulled it in), but the synchronic head is iruska 'be angry at (something)', so causative ruska + -re is bracketed (i-ruska)-re rather than i + ruskare. Kept as a stub so the auto-ingest doesn't overwrite this judgement; see iruskare for the real lemma.
sources: Tommy 1949
notes: Substring artefact; not a standalone morpheme. See iruskare.

composition_note: iruska is intransitive 'be angry (at indef)'; -re adds a causer → 'make someone angry at something'. Bracketing: (i-ruska)-re — ruskare is not an attested independent stem, so we curate iruska as the inner unit.
glosses_jp: 誰かを何かに怒らせる
sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察

composition_note: Ditransitive: ko- (APPL) adds an external goal slot on top of the iruskare base. Bracketing: ko-((i-ruska)-re).
glosses_en: make someone angry at something towards someone
glosses_jp: 誰かに対して誰かを何かに怒らせる
sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察

composition_note: si- coreferences the ko-added goal with one of the higher arguments — 'make someone angry at something towards oneself'. Bracketing: si-(ko-((i-ruska)-re)).
glosses_en: make someone angry at something towards oneself
glosses_jp: 自分に対して誰かを何かに怒らせる
sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察

composition_note: Hierarchical: u- (RECIP) over koiruskare. koiruskare itself decomposes as ko-(iruska-re), and iruska as i-ruska — so the inline etymology view expands the full structure. Avoids treating ``ruskare`` as a standalone morpheme, which it isn't synchronically.
glosses_en: make people angry at something at each other
glosses_jp: 互いに何かに怒らせる
sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察

composition_note: e-（〜について appl）+ yay-（自分 refl）+ ko-（〜に appl）+ puntek（喜ぶ）— 'to rejoice over something for oneself'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Lexicalised surface form ewkoysoytak ← e- + u- (reciprocal) + ko- + isoytak (chat). 'To chat with each other about something'. Phonological metathesis collapses the prefix cluster.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Lexicalised politeness expression — literally i-(INDEF.OBJ) + yay-(REFL) + rayke (kill/thank) + -re (CAUS) 'make oneself thank for something'. Used as the standard polite 'thank you'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Conventionalised greeting. Synchronically opaque to most speakers; the polysynthetic structure is etymological.

composition_note: mina (laugh, vi) + -re (causative) — 'make (someone) laugh', 'amuse'. -re surfaces after the vowel-final stem mina; cf. the seed -e/-re allomorph entries.
sources: Tommy 1949
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: u- (reciprocal) + minare (make laugh, vt) → 'cause each other to laugh' → 'laugh together'. Bracketing: u-(mina-re).
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: si- (indirect reflexive) + kasuy (help) + -re (causative) — 'to make someone help oneself' → 'to get help'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: ram (heart) + pok (under) — 'the area beneath one's heart', idiomatic locus of inward feeling. Curated as an intermediate noun-like unit so rampokwen-class compounds bracket as ((ram-pok)-wen) rather than ram-(pok-wen).
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: pok (under) + ta (locative postp) — 'underneath', 'down below'. Curated as an intermediate locative phrase so pokta-cis brackets as ((pok-ta)-cis) rather than pok-(ta-cis).
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: yay- (REFL.DIR) attached to rampok (beneath the heart) — 'one's own inner being'. Curated as an intermediate unit so eyayrampokwen brackets as e-((yay-rampok)-wen) rather than e-(yay-(rampok-wen)): yay- belongs inside the noun-incorporation host, with wen ('be bad') applying to the whole self-inner-feeling unit.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: e- (APPL) + yayrampok (one's own inner being) + wen (be bad). Bracketing: e-((yay-(ram-pok))-wen). yay- attaches inside the incorporated noun ('self-inner-being') so wen applies to the whole reflexive-possessed noun, then e- adds the applied theme.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: e- (APPL) + yay- (REFL) + pokta (underneath-LOC) + cis (cry). Bracketing: e-(yay-(pokta-cis)). 'To weep at one's own feet over ~' → 'grieve over ~ by oneself'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: riki (high) + kur (body / shadow / figure) — 'high body', a yukar-register expression for one's lofty self or soul.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: kosne (be light) + puni (lift) — 'lift lightly', a yukar-register manner-incorporated compound verb.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: e- (APPL) + yay- (REFL) + rikikur (lofty self) + kosnepuni (lift lightly). Bracketing: e-(yay-(rikikur-kosnepuni)). Yukar-register: 'one lifts one's lofty self into the air'.
glosses_en: float lightly up into the air
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Yukar / epic-poetry register. Multiple incorporated nouns + adverbial roots.

composition_note: ko- (APPL) + sanniyo (think). 'Think about ~' with an applied object.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: e- (APPL) + kosanniyo. Stacked applicative: adds a second oblique-promoted theme on top of ko-sanniyo.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: e- (APPL) + yay- (REFL) + kewtum (heart, incorporated) + ekosanniyo (e-(ko-sanniyo), 'think about'). Bracketing: e-(yay-(kewtum-ekosanniyo)). 'To deeply ponder ~ in one's heart'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Anna Bugaeva's signature extreme example. a= (4.A / 1pl-incl, lowest-priority personal clitic, applied outermost) + e-(yay-(ko-(tuyma-(siram-suypa)))). Literal: 'We keep swaying our hearts afar and toward ourselves over [various things]' → 'we wonder about ~'.
glosses_jp: 我々は～について思い巡らす
sources: ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察
notes: Cited by Bugaeva (2014) as among the most extreme polysynthetic forms in Ainu.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
NINJALCorpus
notes: Adjective-like intransitive verb. Incorporated as a manner modifier in yaykotuymasiramsuypa '思い巡らす' (far-shake-one's-heart).

composition_note: si- (REFL.INDIR) attached to ram (heart) — 'one's own heart'. Curated as an intermediate noun-like unit so larger compounds (yaykosiramsuypa, …) can bracket as tuyma-((si-ram)-suypa) instead of having si- climb outside the noun incorporation.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: yay-「自分」+ ko-「と共に」+ siram「自分の心」+ suypa「（複数で）揺れる」. Bracketing: yay-(ko-(siram-suypa)). The si- + ram pair is bundled as the intermediate noun siram so it stays inside the lexical incorporation, and -pa is treated as the inflectional plural inside suypa, paired with -e in suye.
sources: Wiktionary JA
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Transitive variant of yaykosiramsuypa: e-「について」 adds an external object slot. Bracketing: e-(yay-(ko-(siram-suypa))).
sources: Wiktionary JA

composition_note: Like yaykosiramsuypa but with tuyma「遠い」 incorporated as a manner modifier. Bracketing: yay-(ko-(tuyma-(siram-suypa))).
sources: Wiktionary JA
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: Transitive variant — the paper's signature polysynthetic compound. Bracketing: e-(yay-(ko-(tuyma-(siram-suypa)))). Morphemic gloss: APPL.DAT-REFL-INS-far-(REFL.INDIR-heart)-shake.PL.
sources: Wiktionary JA
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川2024アイヌ語広文典

composition_note: Reciprocal prefix u- attached to koyki: the patient is identified with the (plural) agent → 'fight each other'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

rules.description: Applicative e-: adds an oblique theme as an external argument.

sources: 佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎

notes: Conjunctive particle (接続助詞): joins clauses sequentially ('having V-ed, …'). Listed first so the NINJAL corpus frequency for this dominant sense merges here rather than into the homographic ablative wa.
sources: 佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎

notes: Ablative case particle (格助詞). Distinct from the homographic conjunctive wa 'and' (して), which is the dominant NINJAL sense and is kept as a separate, curated entry under the same lemma.
sources: 佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Suppletive plural counterpart of an 'put.SG'. Tommy 1949 mis-decomposed this as a= + -re (1.A clitic + causative) because of a surface-string accident; ari is a single root.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Etymologically related to ram 'heart, mind' (cf. yaynu, yaykosiramsuypa) but synchronically a single root. The auto-ingest decomposition ram + u- was wrong: u- is a prefix, not a suffix, so it can't appear after the stem.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎
notes: Quotative / similative complementiser. Synchronically opaque — the surface-similar se 'carry' + kor 'have' analysis isn't productive.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Manner deictic. Tommy 1949's e + ne decomposition resolves the bare e to the verb e- 'eat', which is wrong. Plausibly contains the applicative e- + ne 'be', but the analysis is debatable and the form is synchronically opaque.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Distal adnominal demonstrative. The auto-decomposition ne + a= (copula + 1.A clitic) is implausible — a clitic can't appear word-finally in this position. Synchronically a single adnominal.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Proximal adnominal demonstrative. May historically derive from ta + an 'at-which-exists' but synchronically a single adnominal. The auto-decomposition surfaces the etymology, not the synchronic structure.

composition_note: Applicative e- + askay 'be skilled at' → 'be able to (do something)'. Functions as an auxiliary in modal constructions.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎
notes: Earlier Wiktionary-JA ingest pulled in a Japanese-language joke as the gloss; this seed entry overrides it with the standard analysis.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Base for easkay 'be able to'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Numeral 1. Counts both objects (sinep 'one thing') and people (sinen 'one person'). Etymological base of sinen, sinep, sinean, etc.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Numeral 2. Forms tup (tu-p) 'two things' and tun (tu-n) 'two people'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Numeral 3. Distinct from the homonymic -re causative suffix.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Numeral 4. Forms inep (ine-p) 'four things' and inen (ine-n) 'four people'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Numeral 5. Etymology: asik (hand) + -ne (?) — historically 'one hand's worth'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Numeral 8. Etymology: tu (two) + pe-san (subtractive 'minus two' from ten) — 'ten-minus-two'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Numeral 9. Etymology: sine (one) + pe-san — 'ten-minus-one'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Numeral 20. Base of the vigesimal counting system that traditional Ainu uses for larger numbers (e.g. 30 = wan e-tu-hot 'ten before two-twenties').

composition_note: sine 'one' + -p (NMLZ for objects) → 'one thing'. Productive pattern: tup (two things), rep (three things), inep (four things), …
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: kamuy 'god' (incorporated as patient) + nomi 'worship/offer' → 'perform kamuy-nomi'. The central ritual of Ainu religion, in which sake (tonoto) is offered to kamuy with formulaic prayers.
glosses_en: make ritual offering to the gods
glosses_jp: 神々に祈祷の儀式をする
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: kamuy 'god' + cep 'fish' → 'divine fish' = salmon. Salmon is the staple food and one of the most important kamuy in coastal Ainu cosmology.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典
notes: Also called sak ipe 'summer food' or just cep in some dialects.

composition_note: kamuy 'god' + yukar 'epic / chant' → 'kamuy epic'. A genre of oral literature in which the protagonist is a kamuy (god, animal, or spirit) narrating its own adventures in first person. Distinct from human-protagonist yukar.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: kamuy 'god' + hum 'sound/voice' → 'thunder', literally 'the voice of the gods'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: kamuy 'god' + maw 'wind' → 'divine wind / sacred wind'. Often associated with the breath of kamuy in ritual context.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: kamuy 'god' + cikap 'bird' → 'divine bird' = (Blakiston's fish) owl. The owl is one of the most powerful village-protector kamuy in Ainu cosmology.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: kamuy 'god' + kotan 'village' → 'the kamuy world' / 'the divine realm where kamuy dwell after returning from the human world'. Frequently the destination of kamuyhopuni rituals.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: kamuy 'god' + asi (poor / counterfeit) → 'monster' / 'false kamuy'. Used for malevolent or shape-shifting entities that are not true kamuy.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: kamuy 'god/spirit' incorporated as the agent of nukar 'see' → 'be watched over by the gods'. Noun incorporation saturates the agent slot, leaving an intransitive 'someone is watched-over' construction. Standard reverence formula in kamuy yukar.
glosses_en: be watched over by the gods
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察

composition_note: kamuy 'god' + hopuni 'rise up'. In ritual context: 'a god takes off / departs (to the kamuy world)'. Used at the end of kamuy yukar performances.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: wen 'be bad' modifying kamuy 'god/spirit' → 'evil spirit / malevolent kamuy'. Stative-verb attribution: wen functions as an adnominal modifier without becoming a separate clause.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: pirka 'be good' + kamuy 'god' → 'benevolent / good spirit'. Counterpart of wenkamuy.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: aynu 'human' + kamuy 'god' → 'human-god / ancestor spirit'. In Ainu cosmology humans are themselves a kind of kamuy after death; this compound names ancestral / human-spirit kamuy.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: sapa 'head' incorporated as patient of koyki 'catch/strike' → 'be struck on the head / be punished'. Classic example of body-part incorporation cited in Sato 2008.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Body-part noun. Commonly incorporated (sapaha-koyki, sapaun-, sapakar-) and possessable (sapaha 'his/her head').

composition_note: tek 'hand' incorporated as patient of koyki → 'be hit on the hand'. Productive body-part incorporation pattern.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Body-part noun, freely incorporable.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Body-part noun. Distinct from the homophonous sik 'full' (vi). Etymologically related to the bound nu 'eye' that surfaces in nukar 'see'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Plural of pon hi / po. Often appears as a vocative for the speaker's offspring.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: soy 'outside' + ene 'go.toward' → 'go outside'. Lexicalised motion verb.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Poetic / archaic interrogative directional, used in yukar and other oral literature.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Place name appearing in yukar (epic poetry); often the seat of a kamuy or hero.

composition_note: po 'child' + kor 'have' → 'have a child / give birth'. Synchronically a single intransitive verb.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Demonstrative pronoun, distal.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Positional noun; appears in compounds like kanna (above) and as the prefix part of motion verbs.

composition_note: huci 'grandmother' + ape 'fire' — title for the fire goddess (a hearth-deity addressed as 'Grandmother Fire'). Central figure in many kamuy yukar.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
中川1995アイヌ語千歳方言辞典

composition_note: haw 'voice/sound' + ki 'do' → 'say something'. A common alternative to ye 'say' and itak 'speak'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Base for hawki 'say', hawean 'speak / there is a voice', and many discourse nouns.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Backchannel / agreement interjection. The auto-decomp hem + hem is a coincidence; this is a single reduplicated form.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Postpositional locative. Etymologically sam 'side' + -a-ta but lexicalised.

composition_note: kes 'end / every' + to 'day' → 'every day'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: nu 'hear' + -e CAUS → 'cause to hear' = 'tell, inform'. Ditransitive: addresser, addressee, content.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Interrogative locative. Auto-decomposition ne + hi is a surface-string artefact — synchronically a single pronoun.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Intensifying adverb. Synchronically opaque; auto-decomp si- + -no mixes the reflexive prefix and the adverbialiser, which is implausible.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Ethnonym for ethnic Japanese. The surface si + sam substrings are coincidental — not derivable from si- 'reflexive' + sam 'side'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎
notes: Indefinite-person singular pronoun, used as a formal/narrative 1SG (the speaker in yukar epics). The auto-decomp a= + sinuma fails because sinuma is not an independent root.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Loan from Japanese 酒. The auto-decomp sa + -ke is a coincidence with the productive -ke transitivising suffix.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Singular counterpart of rap 'descend.PL'. Auto-decomp ra + -n is wrong — ran is a single root.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Manner interrogative. Possibly historically ne + un but synchronically a single adverb.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Suppletive singular counterpart of arki 'come.PL'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Suppletive plural counterpart of ek 'come.SG'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Singular counterpart of paye 'go.PL'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Stative intransitive verb (formal class 'adjective' in some traditions). Pairs with pon 'be small'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Singular counterpart of ahup 'enter.PL'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Plural counterpart of ahun 'enter.SG'.

田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Suppletive singular counterpart of ari 'put.PL'.

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Typically used of ritual offerings to kamuy (deities/spirits).

sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Base for isoytak (i-soytak 'tell a story'), koisoytak (ko-isoytak 'tell to someone'), ukoisoytak / ewkoysoytak (reciprocal/applicative chains).

composition_note: i- (INDEF.OBJ) + soytak 'tell' → 'tell a story / speak about something'. Overrides the NINJAL auto-decomposition iso + itak, which is a surface-substring artefact: iso 'game animal' + itak 'word' doesn't compose to 'tell a story'.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
佐藤2008アイヌ文法の基礎

composition_note: ko- (APPL.DAT) + isoytak 'tell a story' → 'tell a story to someone'. Surface form koysoytak (with y-epenthesis between ko- and i-) is the same morpheme.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典

composition_note: u- (RECIP) over koisoytak → 'tell stories to each other / converse'. The reciprocal saturates the ko-introduced goal slot. Surface ukoysoytak with the same phonology as koysoytak.
sources: 田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
ブガエワ2014アイヌ語使役構文に関する再考察
田村1996アイヌ語沙流方言辞典
notes: Indefinite/interrogative human pronoun. Auto-decomposition ne + n is opaque; treated as a single root.
